title: Draft initialization handling plan and tool input alignment
summary: Plan: treat an empty tx_event_log.jsonl as “no events yet,” derive baseline tx_state via rebuild without requiring manual tx_event_append, and align tool input expectations by requiring actor (object), payload (object with required fields), and non-empty session_id for tx_event_append plus state for tx_state_save (typically from rebuild/ops_capture_state).
acceptance_criteria: Initialization behavior for empty tx_event_log.jsonl is defined with expected state outcomes.
Required inputs for tx_event_append and tx_state_save are specified for init scenarios.
Any defaults or improved error messaging are scoped to initialization paths.
.rules updates needed for tool input requirements are enumerated.

steps.description: Draft the initialization flow for empty tx_event_log.jsonl, including baseline state behavior.
Document required inputs and any defaults for tx_event_append/tx_state_save in init flows.
List .rules updates needed to enforce tool input requirements.

notes: Init flow: empty log == no events; rebuild should return _init_tx_state and ops_capture_state can persist it.
Tool inputs: tx_event_append requires actor object, payload object (with required fields per event_type), and non-empty session_id; tx_state_save requires a full state object.
Rules update: add explicit required inputs and init guidance without relaxing normal validation.
